senators.name: Katie Britt
senators.notNerdReason: B.A. in political science and J.D. from University of Alabama. Career in Senate staffing, law, and business advocacy. No STEM or technical background. Policy focus on business and family issues rather than evidence-based or data-driven governance.
education: J.D., University of Alabama School of Law
B.A. Political Science, University of Alabama
experience: President and CEO, Business Council of Alabama
Chief of Staff to Sen. Richard Shelby

senators.name: Tommy Tuberville
senators.notNerdReason: B.S. in physical education. Career spent entirely in college football coaching with no policy, technical, or scientific background prior to entering the Senate. No demonstrated engagement with research, data, or evidence-based policy frameworks.
education: B.S. Physical Education, Southern Arkansas University
experience: Head Football Coach, Texas Tech University, 2010-2012
Head Football Coach, University of Cincinnati, 2013-2016

AL-01.name: Barry Moore
AL-01.notNerdReason: B.S. in agricultural science from Auburn University. Background in agriculture and military service (Alabama Army National Guard). Agricultural science degree provides some STEM foundation but limited evidence of applying technical knowledge in legislative work.

AL-02.name: Shomari Figures
AL-02.notNerdReason: B.A. in criminal justice and history, J.D. from University of Alabama. Career in law and government service at DOJ. No STEM or technical background. Policy focus on justice and civil rights rather than data-driven or evidence-based governance.
education: J.D., University of Alabama School of Law
B.A. Criminal Justice and History, University of Alabama
experience: Deputy Chief of Staff and Counselor to U.S. Attorney General
U.S. Department of Justice, Obama and Biden Administrations

AL-03.name: Mike Rogers
AL-03.notNerdReason: B.A. in political science, M.P.A., and J.D. Career in law and politics. As Chair of the House Armed Services Committee, engages with defense technology and procurement data, but background is legal and administrative rather than technical.
education: B.A. Political Science, Jacksonville State University

AL-04.name: Robert Aderholt
AL-04.notNerdReason: B.A. in history and political science, J.D. from Cumberland School of Law. Career in law and politics. Serves on Appropriations Committee but no STEM background or notable evidence-based policy advocacy.
education: J.D., Cumberland School of Law, Samford University
B.A. History and Political Science, Birmingham-Southern College
experience: Assistant Legal Adviser to Governor Fob James

AL-05.name: Dale Strong
AL-05.notNerdReason: B.S. in business administration from Athens State University. Career in county government, firefighting, and emergency services. EMT certification shows practical training but not a STEM or evidence-based policy background.
education: B.S. Business Administration, Athens State University
EMT Certification, University of Alabama in Huntsville

AL-06.name: Gary Palmer
AL-06.notNerdReason: B.S. in operations management from University of Alabama. Led the Alabama Policy Institute for 25 years, showing sustained policy research engagement. Operations management degree has quantitative elements. However, think tank work was ideologically driven rather than evidence-based, and no STEM credentials.
education: B.S. Operations Management, University of Alabama
experience: Worked in engineering and construction firms

AL-07.name: Terri Sewell
AL-07.nerdReason: Exceptionally strong academic credentials: Princeton (B.A. cum laude), Oxford (Marshall Scholar), Harvard Law (J.D.). However, all degrees are in political science and law, not STEM. Career in securities and public finance law before Congress. Data-literate but not technically oriented.
education: M.A., St Hilda's College, Oxford (Marshall Scholar)
B.A. Political Science, Princeton University (cum laude)
experience: Public Finance Attorney, Maynard Cooper & Gale
Judicial Law Clerk, U.S. District Court
